raw_response: {"response":"Do not accept the implementation candidate. It covers the named review primitives, ready/loading/error states, static tests, and desktop/mobile QA, but the source brief makes core accessibility evidence mandatory for a dense custom-control operator screen, and the candidate provides none.","next_action":"Return the candidate for an accessibility evidence pass. Require evidence for keyboard-only operation, focus order and focus visibility, accessible names/roles/states for custom controls, screen reader behavior across queue, detail, decision, and handoff flows, contrast/readability in dense layouts, loading/error announcements, and any automated accessibility findings with fixes.","questions":[],"handoff":"Owner: implementation lead; action: add core accessibility evidence and fix any failures; reason: empty accessibility_evidence blocks acceptance; evidence gap: keyboard, focus, semantic control behavior, screen reader output, contrast, and loading/error accessibility.","rationale":"Passing npm test and desktop/mobile browser QA does not satisfy the accessibility acceptance condition. Because accessibility_evidence is empty, the candidate fails a required evidence gate and should only be reconsidered after the missing evidence is supplied."}

parsed.response: Do not accept the implementation candidate. It covers the named review primitives, ready/loading/error states, static tests, and desktop/mobile QA, but the source brief makes core accessibility evidence mandatory for a dense custom-control operator screen, and the candidate provides none.
parsed.next_action: Return the candidate for an accessibility evidence pass. Require evidence for keyboard-only operation, focus order and focus visibility, accessible names/roles/states for custom controls, screen reader behavior across queue, detail, decision, and handoff flows, contrast/readability in dense layouts, loading/error announcements, and any automated accessibility findings with fixes.
parsed.handoff: Owner: implementation lead; action: add core accessibility evidence and fix any failures; reason: empty accessibility_evidence blocks acceptance; evidence gap: keyboard, focus, semantic control behavior, screen reader output, contrast, and loading/error accessibility.
parsed.rationale: Passing npm test and desktop/mobile browser QA does not satisfy the accessibility acceptance condition. Because accessibility_evidence is empty, the candidate fails a required evidence gate and should only be reconsidered after the missing evidence is supplied.

execution.command_display: codex exec --model gpt-5.5 -c model_reasoning_effort="xhigh" --sandbox read-only --skip-git-repo-check --ephemeral --color never --output-schema <schema> --output-last-message <output> -
